title: Legacy Health Patient Portal Data Settlement – File a Claim by March 16, 2026
amount: $15 cash payment and one free year of CyEx Medical Shield Complete identity and medical monitoring.
description: Legacy Health is settling a class action lawsuit alleging it shared patients' personally identifiable information with Meta (Facebook) and Google without permission — because apparently your medical records weren't private enough. Eligible patients may claim a $15 cash payment and one free year of medical identity monitoring.

title: City of Chicago v DoorDash, Inc., Caviar LLC
description: DoorDash settled with the City of Chicago over allegations that it listed Chicago restaurants on its platform without their consent or contractual agreements, violating a city ordinance requiring written agreements with restaurants. The settlement benefits small Chicago-based restaurants (nine or fewer locations) that were listed on DoorDash or Caviar between August 2019 and November 2025 without a contract. Eligible restaurants can receive up to $2,500 each by submitting an attestation form by the March 30, 2026 deadline.

title: G.Skill DRAM Class Action Settlement 2026 – File a Claim by April 7
description: If you bought G.Skill DDR-4 or DDR-5 desktop memory in the U.S. between January 31, 2018 and January 7, 2026, you may be eligible for a cash payment from a $2,400,000 class action settlement. The lawsuit alleged G.Skill misled customers about the advertised speeds of its memory products.

title: Pacific Life PDX Policy Settlement – Act by April 10, 2026
amount: 3-year term life policy at no cost for terminated policies only
description: If you purchased a Pacific Discovery Xelerator (PDX) indexed universal life insurance policy from Pacific Life Insurance Company in California, a class action settlement over allegedly misleading sales illustrations may entitle you to an automatic credit to your policy's accumulated value or free three-year term life insurance coverage.

title: Panda Restaurant Group Data Breach Settlement – File by April 10, 2026
description: If your personal information was exposed in the March 2023 Panda Restaurant Group data breach, a class action settlement may entitle you to up to $5,000 in reimbursement, a ~$100 cash payment (plus an extra ~$125 for California residents), and two years of credit monitoring.

title: Gryphon Healthcare Data Settlement – File by April 16, 2026
description: Were your medical records exposed in the July 2024 Gryphon Healthcare data breach? If your Social Security number, health insurance information, or personal health data was compromised in this Texas class action settlement, you may qualify for a cash payment. The cyberattack affected patients whose information was stored by Gryphon Healthcare, a medical billing services provider. Submit your claim by April 16, 2026 to receive compensation for the data security incident.

title: Northwell Health Pixel Settlement 2026 – $15 Cash Payment for Eligible Patients
amount: $15 and one year subscription to Privacy Monitoring.
description: Northwell Health agreed to a class action settlement over the use of tracking pixels on its patient portal. Eligible patients who logged into the FollowMyHealth portal or booked appointments between 2020–2024 may qualify for a $15 cash payment. Claims must be submitted by April 20, 2026.

title: PharMerica Data Breach Settlement – File by April 27, 2026
amount: Up to $10,000 or Pro Rata share
description: If your personal or medical information was swept up in the 2023 PharMerica ransomware attack — courtesy of a cybercriminal gang that apparently named themselves "Money Message" — a $5.275 million class action settlement may entitle you to up to $10,000 in reimbursement, a pro rata cash payment, and free credit monitoring.

title: Sprouts Farmers Market FACTA Receipt Settlement – File a Claim by August 5, 2026
description: Sprouts printed too many digits of customers' card numbers on receipts — a FACTA violation. If you used a credit, debit, or EBT card at any U.S. Sprouts store between August 2020 and April 2023, you may be eligible for a cash payment. You must file a claim. Check your email for a notice with a number starting with "P" — that determines which form to use.

title: Tinder Age Discrimination Settlement – California Users Over 28 May Get Cash
description: Tinder allegedly charged older California users more for Plus and Gold subscriptions — just for being older. If you were over 28 or 29 and bought a Tinder subscription in California between 2015 and 2019, you may be owed money. No claim form needed if you got a notice — just pick your payment method by August 18, 2026.

title: Hefty "Recycling" Bags Settlement – Arizona Residents File by October 1, 2026
description: Hefty sold bags labeled "RECYCLING" that Arizona recycling programs don't actually accept — classic greenwashing. The Arizona AG sued, Reynolds settled for $212,000, and now eligible Arizona residents can file a complaint to get a proportional cash payment. No receipt needed. Just five minutes and a mild sense of environmental grievance.

title: Amazon Prime FTC Settlement – File a Claim for Up to $51
description: Amazon settled with the FTC for $2.5 billion over claims it signed people up for Prime without consent and made cancellation a maze. Eligible customers got automatic refunds by December 2025 — but if you didn't, you may still qualify. Check your inbox for a claim notice or visit the settlement site to file.
